name: WorldFirst International Payments API
what_it_does: International business payments and FX platform with REST API — enabling businesses to receive marketplace payments (Amazon, eBay, Walmart), pay international suppliers, and manage multi-currency accounts with competitive exchange rates and API integration.
use_cases: Receiving international marketplace (Amazon, eBay) payouts via World Account API
Paying overseas suppliers in local currency at competitive FX rates via API
Managing multi-currency business accounts with real-time balance queries
Converting currencies programmatically at live market rates via API
Integrating WorldFirst payments into ERP or accounting systems
not_for: Consumer personal remittances (WorldFirst is for businesses)
High-frequency algo trading FX (WorldFirst is for business payments, not trading)
best_when: A cross-border e-commerce business or SME wants competitive FX rates for international supplier payments and marketplace payouts with API integration for accounting.
avoid_when: You need consumer remittance, domestic-only payments, or high-frequency algorithmic FX trading.

auth.notes: OAuth2 Authorization Code for third-party integrations. Access token with refresh. Application ID and secret for OAuth2 flow. Scoped access: account read, payment initiate, FX quote. HMAC signature for webhook verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: FX spread (typically 0.15-1% above mid-market); no monthly fee
pricing.notes: FX spread pricing — no monthly fees. UK-based company, now wholly owned by Alibaba/Ant Group. Particularly strong for China-related trade and Asian supplier payments. Amazon seller community adoption. 100+ currencies. API via partner program registration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for accounts, FX quotes, payments, and transactions. OAuth2 with scopes. Webhook for payment status. API documentation adequate but less comprehensive than Wise or Airwallex. Alibaba ownership may affect API development roadmap. Partner registration required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FX quotes have short validity window (typically 30 seconds). Payment creation supports idempotency via client reference. Account queries idempotent. Currency conversion not idempotent — rates change.
known_agent_gotchas: FX quote validity is short (30 seconds) — execute conversion immediately
Alibaba-owned company — API evolving toward Ant Financial ecosystem integration
Partner registration required before production API access
UK FCA regulated — stricter KYB requirements for business account opening

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. FCA regulated (UK). GDPR compliant. OAuth2 with scopes. HMAC webhook verification. Alibaba/Ant Group ownership — Chinese data governance considerations. UK, HK, SG data residency. Financial services grade security.
